title: Parity: routing (Rust/Zephyr/Python)
description: Policy: three-impl parity. Module: routing. Rust: partial (routing.rs in lichen-rpl). Zephyr: partial (announce.c only). Python: complete (610 LoC hybrid router). Gap: Rust/Zephyr lack unified hybrid routing abstraction combining RPL/Announce/LOADng tiers. Python is reference implementation.
close_reason: Verified gaps confirmed. Filed 6 sub-beads: (1) Rust missing hybrid Router, (2) Zephyr missing LOADng entirely, (3) Zephyr missing hybrid Router, (4) Both missing gradient table, (5) Both missing DTN store-and-forward, (6) Both missing GPSR. Python reference is complete (3,200 LoC routing); Rust has components but no orchestration (2,400 LoC); Zephyr lacks LOADng + orchestration (2,100 LoC).
